ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S "MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED NOTICE OF CLAIMS" IS DENIED. PERMITTING A PLAINTIFF TO AMEND TO SIGNIFICANTLY INCREASE THE AMOUNT OF HER CLAIMS ON THE LAST COURT DAY BEFORE TRIAL WOULD BE AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION. (SEE MURPHY V. KENNETH COLE PRODUCTIONS, INC. (2007) 40 CAL.4TH 1094, 1118.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

Plaintiff'S Motion For Leave To Amend (Set Per Ex Parte Order Filed 9/18/20)
Off Calendar Oct-02-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff'S Motion For Leave To Amend (Set Per Ex Parte Order Filed 9/18/20)

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Drew Lewis (650-665-9243; drew@drewlewis.law) for Plaintiff.
Mike Vartain (mike@vartainlaw.com) for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's "motion for leave to file second amended notice of claims" is denied. Permitting a plaintiff to amend to significantly increase the amount of her claims on the last court day before trial would be an abuse of discretion. (See Murphy v. Kenneth Cole Productions, Inc. (2007) 40 Cal.4th 1094, 1118.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
